Transcrição Completa: How To Attract The Right People - Messaging Mastery
[00:00] Alrighty. So, ladies and gentlemen, we have inner circle member Vlad who is having some issues with his funnel with uh who it's attracting and how it's performing. So, after taking a peek at it, I figured it'd be beneficial for all of us to collectively learn some lessons and get some perspective from this. So, without further ado, let's just dive right in.
[00:25] Okay, so for starters, um Vlad's running a call funnel, and these are the ads to said call funnel. Uh historically, Vlad, you have run the DM strategy, but Vlad was non-compliant. Uh he attempted to convince himself that he knew everything there was to know about compliance, even though he kept getting shut down.
[00:45] And uh yeah, long story short, Vlad, for starters, in the weekly inner circle group call library, probably on one of your pages towards the end there, you'll have the weekly inner circle group calls, there is a specific video that you should absolutely watch on how to say non-compliant things compliantly. And that video specifically would be very beneficial for you.
[01:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Weekly Inner Circle Group Call - September 7th"
NÚMEROS: 09/07
VISUAL: Interface de biblioteca de vídeos, thumbnails de chamadas de grupo, layout de curso online
[01:15] All right. Anyway, so we look at these ads. Uh there's a few things right off the bat that we want to try to figure out. Okay. What I try to look for when we have an offer and in this particular case, the offer is a learn how to sell options premiums.
[01:32] So options premiums are essentially like a form of options. Uh an option premium is the price paid by an options buyer to the seller for the right to buy or sell an underlying asset at a predetermined price within a specific time period. The premium is made up of the options intrinsic value, time value, and volatility value.
[01:55] So as you can see, we have a highly complex topic that has to be simplified through Vlad's offer. He has to somehow articulate that this is the best thing for somebody to do in the trading world comparatively to every other form of trading that there is.
[02:12] Like he has to out compete day trading, prop firms, options trading rather than selling options through uh through premiums. He has to outsell like the buy and hold strategies and indices.
[02:25] The good news is is that the stock industry of just people who already have thoughts about stocks and equities is already in Vlad's favor. Meaning people already have thoughts about it just like they would with real estate, just like they would with all these other offers.
[02:42] So Vlad, you have to find a way to be able to identify in-market demographics. Okay? You have to be able to identify an in-market demographic.

NÚMEROS: 3-4%, 30%, remaining demographics
VISUAL: Diagrama de funil em formato de estádio, segmentação de audiência em camadas concêntricas, 4 níveis de qualificação de prospects
[03:05] I was talking to one of the other inner circle members earlier today, Max. And Max was having a similar issue. Like all his offers looked like they were for somebody that needed convincing. We don't want people who need convinced. Like we want people who are already in market like probable to buy.
[03:25] So this was this was Max's funnel just for some additional perspective on what I'm talking about. So Max teaches people how to buy land, okay? And how to flip land. That's the main offer is like how to flip land outside of being non-compliant for having an income claim uh and a pretty rudimentary guarantee.

[04:08] He then gave me some perspective and said, 'When I say real estate investor, what I'm specifically talking about is somebody who's like trying to get involved in Airbnb or like, you know, they've dabbled in like short-term rentals or flipping or like one form of real estate or another. [04:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Stadium Selling Demographics Breakdown"
NÚMEROS: 3-4% (in-market buyers), 30% (needs convinced), remaining cold demographics
VISUAL: Círculos concêntricos representando níveis de qualificação, cores diferenciadas por temperatura de lead, setas indicando progressão de convencimento
[04:55] The main perspective that we want to share here, okay? And this is important to note. I'll visualize it for you guys, is remember that stadium selling logic that Chad Holmes describes, okay?
[05:08] So, here we are pitching people on stuff that we want to sell them. The smallest demographic that's the hottest demographic is that 3 to 4% of in-market buyers. That's where the fucking money is. Okay.
[05:22] From there, the second largest demographic is typically made up of a 30 percentile. That is a needs convinced demographic. The other two demographics are an even colder version of somebody who needs convinced. So, they need convinced even more. And then this demographic just never fucking buys.
[05:42] Okay? So, we always want to find a way to be able to successfully communicate to this 3 to 4%. When we scale, right? meaning after we literally tapped out of the 3 to 4%. That's where this needs convinced demographic comes in.
[06:00] So if you're struggling like if you have like a strugglesome show rate if like meaning like Max's show rate as an example without senior an errant amount is just below average. Okay, well below what the statistic would be if he was targeting and communicating to an in-market demographic.
[06:22] So generally when any conversion mechanism that you have a call funnel, a webinar funnel, a challenge funnel, a DM strategy etc. Whenever any variation of a conversion mechanism fails, it is generally a who is coming into it is in that needs convinced category.
[06:42] Like you haven't pivoted your messaging enough to be able to find, you know, the right type of way to communicate.

[12:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Culture Index - Circles, Triangles, Squares"
NÚMEROS: 3 tipos de personalidade
VISUAL: Diagrama de formas geométricas representando diferentes estilos de processamento de informação: Circles (big picture), Triangles (chunked information), Squares (analytical details)
[13:05] Okay? None. All it articulates is the vision. Okay? Rich people operate on vision. They operate on big perspectives. There was this company. We've we've talked about this a lot actually in the inner circle. It's called Culture Index.
[13:20] Okay. And Culture Index describes people as circles, triangles, and squares. Okay. And when they initially break down this lesson, they're saying like usually the executives are circles and like the business owners are circles.
[13:35] And circles think in like the grandest picture. Okay, so a circle would look at like the overarching big picture. They get lost in the sauce when you start talking about technicals, tactics, like individual actions. They prefer like the big picture.
[13:52] Okay, you got triangles. Triangles are generally like management. Um these can also be individual employees to be fair. But what triangles do is they like chunk everything. So to understand the big picture, they need it in chunks.
[14:08] And once they understand it in like this, bing, then the whole picture becomes visible to them. If they don't view it in chunks, then they won't be able to see the whole picture at all.
[14:20] And then you got squares. Okay, squares are like highly analytical people. Squares are people who look at the picture like this. Like they can't even see the big picture, dude. Like they are in the fucking trenches.
[14:35] Like they're like farsighted looking at everything like super up close in order to see anything. And again, rarely do they ever actually have the big picture in mind. They just are so like individual tactic oriented.
[14:50] These are the people, as an example, like if I make a video talking about like some big shit for a business owner, these types of fucking nerds would comment on the video and be like, "Bro's just yapping." You like, "You didn't even say anything." Because they can't see the fucking big picture, you know?

[15:38] Okay, the circles generally get a little bit like eh I don't like what's going on when you communicate in the triangle form. The triangle people are so used to having to hear the way that the circles like things that they've learned to adapt and like break down these categories in their own way.
[15:55] Like you don't need to communicate to triangles. You can communicate essentially to circles exclusively in order for the to in order to effectively communicate to triangles as well.

[23:55] Yeah. So, how do I say this? Not be a dick. You you have to be able to communicate like the circumstances of somebody that's doing 125k already a year in those first couple seconds. Like that's the most important way to start an ad.
[24:12] You have to start an ad by communicating the circumstances of the individual you're talking to. So like dude, 125k a year is in the top like I think 3% of all income earners in the United States.
[24:28] Like it'd be better to start the video with something along the lines of like so you're already doing well in your job and you've tried to pick up trading and you're looking for the thing that can finally work. You might have tried day trading. You might have tried investing and picking individual companies and at the end of the day like staring at charts, executing trades and waiting for that perfect opportunity has just not worked out up to this point and taken too much time.
[24:55] I was there too and that's why I created this paycheck collector strategy. I leverage something that I previously viewed as rather complex in the options trading world that was called options premiums and I simplified it down into a system that worked for me and gave me a very consistent return without a lot of time invested and without more importantly having to sit around and just wait for the perfect trade.
[25:20] It didn't take a lot and I really enjoyed this strategy. As I grew more and more consistent and profitable with it through time, I started getting questions about it and therefore I turned around and started teaching.
[25:35] In your ad copy, you say something about over 432 professionals like you have turned their trading around with this strategy. Then you could say, I've had 432 other people trust me to learn this strategy that get consistent results with it as well. And you're next.
[25:52] If you're interested, I put together a video on how it works. Click the link below to find out more. It's like again when you articulate at the very very very very very beginning of the video like the the circumstances of the right people, you don't have to say shit like, "Are you currently making more than 125k per year?"
[26:15] All that really signals is that you're doing awful like the worst with communicating effectively to that person. That's a perfect example of why I started this video talking about messaging.
[26:28] Like the biggest lesson in marketing that gets shit to actually work is being able to dial in a proper way of communicating to the people you actually want to get through your funnel.

[27:22] See, and like here's the thing, Vlad. Like, this is not how fucking real people. That's like not even remotely close to how a fucking real person's life is, dude. It's like you got to be tapped into reality, okay?
[27:38] Reality is important to understand. Like, people who work jobs, they're not sitting there thinking to themselves, like who the fuck sits there and just dwells in pain? People who kill themselves, like human beings, there's a great book you should read. It's called Vital Lies and Simple Truths.
[27:55] Human beings hate pain so much that instead of actually digging into the reasons that the pain exists, they go to extreme lengths to create false worlds to operate in just so they're out of pain.
[28:10] Okay? Like humans don't just sit around all day and think, "Yeah, man. I fucking hate my job. I'm sitting around all day." Like, who the fuck hates their job in most instances? Broke people. You know who loves their job? People doing financially well. That's who loves their fucking job.
[28:28] People who want consistency, comfort, security. Like, there's a reason that these people have jobs in the first place is be especially well-paying jobs like making 125k or more a year.
[28:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Real Circumstances of $125K+ Earners"
NÚMEROS: $125K+ annual income
VISUAL: Descrição de lifestyle real: casa própria, kids em extracurriculares, orgulho do trabalho, vida confortável - não pain e suffering
[28:50] The real circumstances of somebody who's making 125k or more a year are that they have a comfortable life, that they probably live in some house that they likely own, that again they've worked hard to own, and that they're prideful in owning. They potentially have kids.
[29:08] Their kids play sports are in some kind of extracurricular activities and the parent prides themselves on the fact that they get to spend time with their kids outside of work that maybe they get to take their kids to school.
[29:22] You know, they don't fucking dwell on the fact that their life sucks because they work a job. That's so out of touch. And again, that's a huge reason why you're attracting terrible people to your funnel because you're not articulating the real circumstances of somebody making 125K a month.
[29:42] This specific ad would sound more like, "Look, you got a great life. You worked hard for the home that you've managed to purchase. You love your job. Things are going great, and you're just looking for a way to get even more out of life, but you don't want it to take that much more time.
[30:00] You want to go to the games that your kids have. You like going to their extracurriculars. You like spending time doing what you love to do outside of your work. And you need a way to trade that's not going to take a lot of time from you.
[30:15] Day trading requires you to sit around and wait for the perfect moment to execute the trade. Plus, on top of that, you got to pick the right vehicle for what you're going to trade to make it work. You got to be tapped into the news. It's a lot. It's exhausting. And that's likely why you haven't figured that out yet.
[30:35] A better vehicle that worked for me and 432 of my students who have trusted me is learning how to sell options premiums. It comes off as rather complex when you first learn about it, but I found a way to simplify it down to help you easily understand how this could impact your life positively.
[30:52] It doesn't take a lot of time. Matter of fact, I put together a short video on the next page to give you some perspective on how it might work for you and some of the expectations for what it would take to get started with it in a very realistic way.
[31:08] If you're looking for an opportunity to just make a little more to get that extra whatever you're looking for with extra money right now, got a great opportunity for you that I think is extremely consistent and will work great for you.
[31:22] You know, like dude, that is a fucking way to communicate somebody making a good income through a job. You know what I mean? Not like this comparatively.
[31:35] [VLAD'S AD CONTINUAÇÃO] "You've worked hard to build a career making 125K or more. But let me ask you something. What's it really costing you? Is it the time you're missing with your family? The freedom to actually enjoy the life you built? Trading options isn't about just making more money. It's about taking control. It's about replacing the stress of uncertainty with the security of financial freedom with the paycheck collector strategy."
[32:00] See what I mean? Like, dude, the people you're communicating with right there are fucking brokies. Like, you know who experiences that? Broke people.

[32:22] Yeah, let's watch another.

VISUAL: Análise de transição abrupta entre problem e solution, falta de ponte emocional/lógica
[33:32] There's like a bridge missing on this one. You know, like the first part is all right. "Let me guess. You've tried trading, lost money, and now you're wondering if it's even possible to win."
[33:45] See, so like right there, that's the first part, and then right here, you would need a bridge. Like versus watch how like abruptly you just change to the other side, which sounds extremely similar.
[33:58] It's almost as if you're like, "Hey, so you were in a relationship and the guy hit you. Uh, let me tell like here's the deal. Um, I'm a guy who wants to be in a relationship and I would love to take you on a date."
[34:12] It's like there's a missing bridge there that like comforts the person and tells them that your way is different. You know, really look for like how abrupt this transition is in this one.
[34:25] "Let me guess. You've tried trading, lost money, and now you're wondering if it's even possible to win. Here's the deal. NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: High friction question emocional, desalinhada com messaging para $125K+ earners
[1:00:25] Okay. When did you last feel completely confident in your financial future, dude? Like it just doesn't make sense to me. The ads. Okay. Hey, you're 125k a month. Uh I'm sorry. 125k a year. Sorry, I'm so talking so used to talking about that amount in months.
[1:00:45] Anyway, okay. So, you're talking to people make 125k per year, right? And then in your VSSL, you completely just say, "Fuck that person." And just say, "Hey, if you're a business owner, an executive, right?"
[1:01:00] And then here in this question, you're just like, "How long have you been uh completely confident in your financial future?" Like, "When was the last time?" You know, again, it's like, "Is this a leverable question in a sales call?" It could be, but to me, it just feels like a high friction question that your sales people likely aren't using.
[1:01:22] I bet if I secret shop your sales team, I bet they don't use this question. I bet they don't. I don't. It doesn't never come up.
[1:01:32] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Application Question 6: 12 Months From Now..."
NÚMEROS: 12 months
VISUAL: "Duck question" - resposta óbvia que todo mundo quer (mais dinheiro, vida melhor, etc.)
[1:01:38] Okay. 12 from 12 months from now. It's like, dude, everybody fucking It's again, it's like an obvious answer, right? It's like, why put these fucking duck questions in here? It's Yeah, everybody 12 months from now wants to make more money, have a fucking sexier life, you know, be fitter, hotter, better, fucking more financial. It's like a fucking duck question, dude. You know what I mean?
[1:02:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Application Questions 7-8: Occupation & Income"
NÚMEROS: $125K annually, $40K monthly (Inner Circle example)
VISUAL: Pergunta de income qualification que pode afastar big dogs, exemplo do Inner Circle onde $40K/month minimum afastou high earners
[1:02:08] Okay. What's your occupation? Sure, that's fine. Uh, do you make over 125K annually? Yeah, sure. Like again, that's fine. I'll give you some good perspective, though.
[1:02:22] So, one time, this was a couple weeks ago, there was a guy who wanted to join into the inner circle, and he hits me up and he goes, "Hey, Jeremy, do you have like the higher level version of the inner circle?" The higher level version? What are you talking about? There's guys in there making millions of dollars a month.
[1:02:42] And he's like, "Oh, well, in your application, you said you have to make 40k a month minimum." So, I just assumed it was a bunch of brokies, not like me. And I was like, honestly, fair point, you know, fair point.
[1:02:58] There's guys in that very group alongside you right now that you are watching this video with that make a fuckload of money, literally millions of dollars a month. Okay? The smallest people in there are are like at a minimum in most instances like 50 to 80k a month at like the smallest minority fraction of the group.
[1:03:20] That question of do you make 40k a month turns off the big dogs. I bet I have lost people I'll never know who are like oh I bet this guy could help me. And they go through my application and they're like 40k a month it's for fucking small fries. You get what I mean?
[1:03:38] It's like this question right here. Although you because you attract broke people to your through your funnel because you you've been doing terrible at messaging, you ask this question and it and to be fair, it can be a qualifier. Okay, obviously it's a qualifier. I'm sure you disqualified people who answer this question with no.
[1:03:58] But again, my point is is like if you just ask the question of do you have this amount of money for the program? Yes, I can get approved for funding or no, that that can be the real qualifier and you can take this out and then you do actually attract the more financially qualified people as a result of not having this question in there. That's the main point I'm trying to make.
[1:04:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Application Final Question: Will You Show Up?"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Pergunta inútil para no-show rate, checkbox "I'm honest and will keep my word"
[1:04:28] Okay? And then contact information. And from there, you ask the classic, I have a no-show rate problem question. Will you show up for my call? I'm honest and will keep my word. Oh, dude.
[1:04:42] Remember, when you have a no-show rate issue, you have a messaging problem and you're likely targeting the needs convinced demographic. If you are at scale watching this video, okay? And I mean at scale where you're you need to be targeting the needs convinced demographic and you have a show rate problem, this question is fucking worthless.
[1:05:05] It never does anything. It in no way, shape, or form has any impact. Literally, statistically at all from testing. I'm not just sitting here ragging on you. I'm literally telling you this question is truly meaningless. It has never done anything to improve show rate ever.
[1:05:25] You know what improves show rate? The hammer them strategy. In-market demographics. Making people give a fuck and selling them to show up to the call through the follow-up material that happens after they book a call. Okay? That's what makes people show up.

[1:06:52] I gave the example earlier today in the inner circle chat of a call confirmation page we saw a lot of success with that follows a similar strategy. The one thing I would encourage you to do, you'll notice here we have the desktop version of the make sure you press the I know the sender option.
[1:07:10] If you turn it into mobile, it looks like the mobile version. We always start off our call confirmation pages like that. That's a good pro tip because again, Google has limited the um calendar notification going directly into people's calendars, even if they booked the call.
[1:07:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Breakout Videos Size Comparison"

It comes off as like very long, you know, but again, I'm not like fucking you here. Like, you did a good job on on having this amount of videos. Okay, that's good.
[1:07:58] And by the way, they show up damn near the same on mobile. Like you'll see on mobile how they're all stacked like this. Like yours on mobile looks about the same. You see? So, it's like they're a little bit bigger, but again, on mobile, it's like you're damn near doing the exact same thing. Why not just clean it up a little bit on desktop?
[1:08:20] But anyway, uh then you got the pre-call checklist, you know, and then you just litter it with testimonials, which again, you did well. You got all your litter littered testimonials down there.
[1:08:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Core Problem: Messaging"
NÚMEROS: 3-4% in-market demographic
VISUAL: Resumo final: todo o problema é messaging, necessidade de identificar in-market demographic real
[1:08:42] So, you just got a big messaging issue. Anybody watching this video, that's the point I'm trying to make is like I gave you many examples throughout this video of how to dial in messaging, how to get messaging to work. You know, why messaging is as important as it is.
[1:09:00] You have to fix the messaging. The messaging is what gets it to function well. That is the key, ladies and gentlemen, is dialing in messaging. And and remember, it's like, dude, at Vlad's scale, why would he not target the in-market demographic?
[1:09:20] You have And if you're not aware, like if you're just sitting here questioning like, well, what the fuck even is my in-market demographic? That's the fucking question that gets you to a much better financial position than you're in now, my friend. And that'd be a great question to dwell on as this video finishes up.
[1:09:42] All right, good luck out there.
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
3-4% = in-market buyers (hot demographic)
30% = needs-convinced demographic
77% drop-off entre 3 e 15 segundos em ads
17% play rate em VSSLs
23% completion rate em VSSLs
$125K+ annual income = top 3% US earners
$75K - $900K = range de investimento Lux Locker
28% = retorno anualizado cash-on-cash Lux Locker
$1.6 billion = venda para Barclays (exemplo)
432 students vs 1,000 clients = inconsistência numérica de Vlad
50% completion rate = melhor cenário possível (vídeo 1 min)
15% play rate = vídeo oferecendo 12 lead magnets gratuitos
$40K/month minimum = threshold que afasta big dogs (Inner Circle example)
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Stadium Selling Logic (Chad Holmes): Segmentação em 4 níveis - 3-4% in-market, 30% needs-convinced, cold demographics, never buyers
Circle Talk: Comunicação big picture para high-income prospects, evitar technicals
Culture Index: Circles (big picture), Triangles (chunked info), Squares (analytical details)
Hammer Them Strategy: 6 emails/dia para educar prospects pós-booking
Messaging Hierarchy: Comunicar circumstances primeiro, bridge para solution, social proof
VSSL Structure: Storytelling > income claims, journey narrative, testimonials como average results
Application Best Practices: Easy first question, price qualification direta, leverage questions only
Confirmation Page: Multiple breakout videos, calendar instructions (desktop/mobile), testimonials
ESTRUTURA:
In-Market Demographic Identification: Definir quem já está pronto para comprar vs precisa ser convencido
Messaging Alignment: Ads → VSSL → Application → Confirmation deve ser consistente
Visual Branding: Wistia Pro (remove logo, custom colors, video thumbnails), symmetry, color matching
Copy Framework: Circumstances → Problem → Bridge → Solution → Social Proof → CTA
Disqualification Strategy: Disqualifiers claros no VSSL, não income gates no application
Show Rate Optimization: Messaging correto + Hammer Them + in-market targeting (não checkbox promises)
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Options premiums = veículo de Vlad
$125K+ annual = target income de Vlad
Lux Locker: $75K-$900K investment, 28% annual return, 2-year average hold
Inner Circle: $40K/month minimum afasta prospects de milhões/mês
Pricing disclosure: deve ser direta no application, não inflada com "top echelon" language
PROBLEMAS IDENTIFICADOS EM VLAD:
Messaging para broke people: Pain-focused ads atraem losers, não $125K+ earners
Inconsistência de target: Ads = employees, VSSL = business owners/executives
Visual scammy: Red text, mean thumbnail, asymmetria, default Wistia blue
Income claims excessivos: Atrai brokies, não sophisticated investors
Application friction: "Did you watch video?", high-friction emotional questions
Falta de storytelling: Teleprompter reading vs authentic journey narrative
Números conflitantes: 432 vs 1,000 clients
EXEMPLOS POSITIVOS:
Lux Locker: Circle talk, vídeos 30-45s, big picture vision, dressed the part
Max's pivot: De needs-convinced para in-market (land flipping advantages)
Vlad's confirmation page: Multiple breakout videos (excellent practice)
DURAÇÃO: 1:09:42 FORMATO: Screen share + live funnel critique + examples comparison
